publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-04 00:03:19 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: James J. Eyster papers
did.abstract: Course materials for courses; research reports; a student housing project study from the University of Pennsylvania; presentation and consulting notes for various hotels; research interview notes; publications; lecture notes and the Homeless Program Summary.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of Hotel Administration, Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Course materials for courses in Financial Analysis and Planning, Hospitality Financial Management, Integrated Case Studies in the Hospitality Industry, and Financial Accounting; research reports, primarily from the firm of Laventhol & Horwath; a student housing project study from the University of Pennsylvania; presentation and consulting notes for various hotels; research interview notes; publications; lecture notes and the Homeless Program Summary.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: James J. Eyster papers, #28-2-2327. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Bars (Drinking establishments) -- Finance.
Taverns (Inns) -- Finance.
Hotels -- Finance.
Hotel management schools.
Hotel management -- Study and teaching.
Homeless persons.
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. School of Hotel Administration

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Professional History Summary - James J. Eyster
II.a. Books
The Negotiation and Administration of Hotel Management Contracts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: School of Hotel Administration. First printing, January 1977; second printing, March 1977; third printing, April 1978; fourth printing, March 1979. Reviews of the Book appeared in Real Estate Review (Spring 1978) and Hotel and Motel Management (April 1977).

did.unittitle: The Negotiation and Administration of Hotel Management Contracts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Revised Edition, School of Hotel Administration, November 1980. Complete revision of data base from first edition and addition to two major chapters

did.unittitle: Financing the Lodging Industry: Players and Techniques of the 1980s
The Negotiation and Administration of Hotel Management Contracts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Third Edition, School of Hotel Administration, October 1987. Revision and expansion of 1980 edition.

did.unittitle: The Negotiation and Administration of Hotel Management Contracts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Japanese language edition, translated by Yuji Yamaguchi, Shibata Publishing Company, Tokyo, Japan, 314 pages

did.unittitle: Hotel Management Contracts in the U.S.: The Revolution Continues and Twelve Areas of Concern

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A supplement to The Negotiation and Administration of Hotel and Restaurant Management Contracts, Cornell University School & Hotel Administration, Ithaca, NY

did.unittitle: II.b. Articles
The Hotel-Motel Feasibility Study: What the Investor Should Demand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cornell Quarterly, November 1973, pp. 6-12. Review of this article appeared in The Market Information Guide (February 1974). The article was reprinted by The Cornell Quarterly as part of its series entitled "The Hotel-Motel Feasibility Study."

did.unittitle: Insuring Your Profit Margin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cornell Quarterly, November 1974, pp. 25-33. Review of this article appeared in Nation's Restaurant News (September 18, 1978). The article was reprinted by The Cornell Quarterly for student and professional use.

did.unittitle: Economic Development and Public Policy: Tourism as an Alternative
Factors Influencing the Adoption of Management Contracts in the Lodging Industry
Owner and Operator Bargaining Power Factors in Hotel Management Contract Negotiations
Negotiating the Provisions of Concern in Hotel Management Contracts
Implications of the Management Contract
Negotiating the Hotel and Motor Inn Management Contract
Administering the Hotel Management Contract: An Analysis of Owner and Operator Concerns
Major Concerns During Management Contract Negotiations
Where Do We Go From Here? The Future of the Management Contract
The Hotel Management Contract: Owner and Operator Concerns
Operational Budgeting in Small Hotel Companies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cornell Quarterly, pp. 80-84. Co-authored with Jorge G. Kosturakis.

did.unittitle: How to Negotiate a Contract, Part I

Methoden in der Beherbergungsindustrie

scopecontent.p: Allgemeine Hotel-und Gaststatten-Zeitung/Deutsche Hotel-Nachrichten. Adopted and translated from "The Capital Investment Decision," published in The Cornell Quarterly. Co-authored with A. Neal Geller.
